steps.title: Identify the logical goal
steps.content: The passage states that coins with silver content below 80% were considered unsuitable for trade, causing a crisis of confidence. Abd'astart I's decision must logically restore silver content above 80% to relieve the crisis. The 367 BCE coins had 74.2% silver; restoring confidence requires pushing silver percentage back above 80%.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'keep the amount of silver in Sidonian coins consistent with that in coins minted in 367 BCE but decrease their weight': Holding the absolute silver constant while reducing total weight raises the silver percentage above 74.2% — back above the 80% threshold, restoring trade confidence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'proclaim that the percentage of silver in coins suitable for trade would be raised to a threshold higher than 80%': A proclamation about a future standard does not actually fix current coins' silver content or restore confidence in existing currency. ✗
  • Choice C — 'fund the mining of some copper deposits that were not available to Ba'alsillem II': Adding more copper would worsen debasement and further reduce the silver percentage, the opposite of what is needed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'begin minting heavier coins with a proportion of silver to copper similar to that in coins minted in 367 BCE': Maintaining the same 74.2% silver proportion in heavier coins keeps silver content below 80%, failing to resolve the crisis. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
